values.label: Comprehensive Somatic Tumor Profiling — 500+ Gene Panel
Comprehensive Solid Tumor DNA + RNA Panel
Malignant neoplasm of upper-outer quadrant of left female breast
Malignant neoplasm of right kidney, except renal pelvis
Acute myeloid leukemia, without mention of having achieved remission
Encounter for adjustment and management of vascular access device
Oral mucositis (ulcerative) due to antineoplastic therapy
Malignant neoplasm of head of pancreas

Planned primary debulking surgery: exploratory laparotomy, TAH-BSO, omentectomy, appendectomy, bilateral pelvic and para-aortic lymph node dissection, peritoneal implant resection, diaphragmatic stripping; R0 resection achieved; overnight SICU then floor
Febrile neutropenia (ANC 0.3 K/uL, temperature 38.9 C) after cycle 1 carboplatin/cabazitaxel; IV piperacillin-tazobactam, 2 units pRBCs for grade 3 anemia, transient AKI
Grade 3 immune-mediated colitis after cycle 3 ipilimumab/nivolumab; treated with IV methylprednisolone and infliximab
Grade 3 diarrhea requiring IV hydration and electrolyte repletion during FOLFIRINOX cycle 7
Grade B pancreatic fistula post-Whipple: IV antibiotics, TPN, octreotide, CT-guided drain repositioning
Symptomatic anemia (Hgb 8.9 g/dL) in setting of olaparib progression with hepatic metastases; transfusion support and goals of care discussion
Port-a-cath placement for chemotherapy infusion access
Planned stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife Icon) to three brain metastases; outpatient same-day procedure; patient tolerated well
Subcutaneous implanted venous access port placement (right chest wall, subclavian approach) prior to chemotherapy initiation

AML-MRC induction chemotherapy (7+3 cytarabine/daunorubicin plus midostaurin); complicated by febrile neutropenia with E. coli bacteremia, grade 3 mucositis; post-induction bone marrow biopsy confirming CR1
Grade A pancreatic fistula recurrence during adjuvant GemCap cycle 1; managed conservatively with NPO, drain management, and octreotide; gemcitabine held
Stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife) to three brain metastases; planned outpatient procedure requiring brief overnight observation
Port-a-cath placement for chemotherapy venous access prior to cycle 1 initiation
Grade 3 irinotecan-induced diarrhea and Grade 3 neutropenia (ANC 0.6) after cycle 6 of FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab; managed with IV fluids, loperamide, empiric antibiotics, and filgrastim
Planned left partial mastectomy (lumpectomy) with wire localization, sentinel lymph node biopsy, and axillary lymph node dissection levels I-II

left base of tongue, left level IIA cervical lymph node
